one_liner: Datadog offers a broad, self-serve REST API (v1/v2) authenticated via API/application keys or personal/service access tokens, with an official MCP server enabling AI agents to query observability data directly.

auth_methods.notes: DD-API-KEY header required for write access; DD-APPLICATION-KEY header additionally required for read access on many endpoints.
Personal Access Tokens (PATs) or Service Access Tokens (SATs) can be used as Bearer token in Authorization header or via dd-application-key header, in place of API key.

self_serve_notes: Free tier available (up to 5 hosts, 1-day metric retention) plus a 14-day free trial of the full platform; API keys and application keys can be generated self-serve from the account dashboard without needing sales contact. Full feature usage (e.g., Synthetic Monitoring) is metered/billed on top of paid plans.

api_surface.notes: Extensive REST API (v1/v2) covering metrics, logs, monitors, dashboards, incidents, synthetics, and more; official client libraries in multiple languages.

evidence.supports: Describes DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY header authentication.
Confirms REST API structure, JSON responses, authentication via API key and application key, and official client libraries.
Describes Personal Access Tokens as alternative auth method usable as Bearer token.
Describes Service Access Tokens as alternative auth method.
Confirms self-serve signup for free trial.
Details free tier and pricing structure.
Indicates existence of official Datadog MCP tooling.
